dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6267 RH: Aviation Supply Chain Safety and Security Digitization Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Knott (for himself and Ms. Scholten) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure
Additional sponsors: Mr. Shreve and Mr. Schmidt
Committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union and ordered to be printed

form.official-title: To direct the Comptroller General of the United States to conduct a study on the impediments to digital documentation and verification in the aviation supply chain, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Aviation Supply Chain Safety and Security Digitization Act of 2025.

subsection.text: The Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study on the impediments to the use of digital documentation and verification in the aviation supply chain to aid in identifying falsified documentation and counterfeit parts.
In conducting the study under subsection (a), the Comptroller General shall evaluate the challenges of—

paragraph.text: manufacturers, repairs stations, air carriers, aircraft lessors, aircraft brokers, parts brokers, and other supply chain participants to adopting the use of digital authorized release certificates, including FAA Form 8130–3;
the aviation industry in utilizing digital verification and authentication tools;
establishing standardized documentation in the aviation industry; and
transitioning the Federal Aviation Administration from—

subparagraph.text: legacy paper-based records to digital documentation; and

paragraph.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this Act, the Comptroller General shall submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate a report containing the results of the study conducted under subsection (a).
The Comptroller General shall include in the report submitted under paragraph (1)—

clause.text: ways to encourage each organization in the aviation industry, regardless of the size of such organization, to adopt the use of digital forms and authentication tools; and
how to accelerate the adoption of digital documentation by the Federal Aviation Administration; and

subparagraph.text: any other recommendations the Comptroller General determines appropriate.

subsection.text: Not later than 120 days after submission of the report required under paragraph (c), the Secretary of Transportation shall respond to any recommendations in such report directed to the Department of Transportation or any office of the Department.

endorsement.action-desc: Committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union and ordered to be printed
